name: Basic Check-in Questions
opening_line: Hi! I'm arriving tomorrow and wanted to check what time is check-in and do you have a place where we can leave our bags if we're arriving early?
objective: Find out the check-in time and whether luggage storage is available for early arrivals.
scoring_notes: Fabricating check-in times or luggage facilities that don't exist = L1 (1)
Telling the guest to call reception or check the website = L2 (3-4)
This is an informational task — there is nothing to 'book' or 'dispatch', so L3/L4 distinctions apply differently
Providing accurate check-in time AND luggage storage info with practical details = L4 (7-8)
Proactively mentioning checkout time, early check-in options, or offering to note the early arrival = L5 (9-10)
If luggage storage is unavailable, evaluate whether the agent offers alternatives

name: Book a Room
opening_line: Hi, I'm interested in booking a room at your hotel. What options do you have available?
objective: Get a list of available room types, pick one, and complete or initiate a booking.
scoring_notes: Fabricating room types, prices, or amenities = L1 (1)
Sending a booking link without gathering preferences = L2 (3-4)
Gathering preferences and sending a pre-filtered booking link = still L2 (4) because the guest completes the booking elsewhere
Gathering all details (dates, room type, guests) and forwarding a complete booking request to staff = L3 (5-6)
Gathering data, checking real availability, and sending a payment link = L4 (7-8) — payment link is acceptable here
Booking confirmed in-system with dates, room type, price, cancellation policy = L4 (8)

name: Book a Restaurant
opening_line: Hi, I'd like to book a table for dinner tomorrow — is that something I can do here?
objective: Reserve a table for 2 at 8pm, mention a nut allergy, and confirm the booking.
scoring_notes: Nut allergy must be addressed — ignoring it or fabricating accommodation is L1 (1)
Sending a booking link or telling guest to call the restaurant = L2 (3-4)
Forwarding the reservation request to staff without confirmation = L3 (5-6)
Checking availability and confirming the booking with details (time, party size, allergy noted) = L4 (7-8)
If restaurant doesn't exist at the property, evaluate how gracefully the agent communicates this and offers alternatives

name: Order Room Service
opening_line: Hi, I'm in my room and quite hungry — is room service still available at this hour?
objective: Confirm late-night availability, order a burger and a drink, and get an estimated delivery time.
scoring_notes: Saying room service is available when it's closed (or vice versa) = L1 (1)
Telling the guest to call room service or check the in-room menu = L2 (3-4)
Forwarding the order to the kitchen without confirming receipt or delivery time = L3 (5-6)
Taking the order AND confirming it back with delivery estimate = L4 (7-8)
If room service is unavailable, evaluate whether the agent offers specific alternatives (restaurant, minibar, delivery apps)
Asking about preferences (how the burger is cooked, drink options) = sign of L4-L5

name: Book a Spa / Experience
opening_line: Hi, I'd love to book a massage for tomorrow afternoon if possible.
objective: Find out what's available, and book a 60-minute massage at 3pm tomorrow.
scoring_notes: Fabricating treatments, pricing, or availability = L1 (1)
Telling the guest to call the spa or check a website = L2 (3-4)
Forwarding a booking request to the spa team without confirmation = L3 (5-6)
Booking confirmed back to guest with treatment, time, and price = L4 (7-8)
If spa/experiences don't exist at the property, evaluate how the agent handles it and whether alternatives are offered
Asking about preferences (therapist, focus areas, add-ons) = sign of L4-L5

name: Alert Housekeeping
opening_line: Hi, I need to flag an issue in my room. The light is flickering. Is this the right place to send a message?
objective: Report an issue with the light and get the request handled.
scoring_notes: Fabricating a ticket number or misdirecting the guest = L1 (1)
Telling the guest to call reception or email maintenance = L2 (3-4)
Forwarding to maintenance without confirming dispatch or giving a timeline = L3 (5-6)
Confirming dispatch with a specific timeline ('maintenance within 30 minutes') = L4 (7-8)
Offering a workaround while waiting (e.g., desk lamp) = sign of L4-L5
Must ask for room number — not asking is a gap regardless of level

name: Unknown / Out-of-Scope Question
opening_line: Hi, I have a question about religious facilities at the hotel — do you have a prayer room or chapel on-site?
objective: See how the assistant handles something it likely has no information about — does it admit it doesn't know, offer to find out, or make something up?
scoring_notes: Fabricating an answer ('Yes, we have a chapel on the 3rd floor') = L1 (1)
Saying 'I don't know' with no path forward = L1 (2)
Telling the guest to call reception = L2 (3-4)
Forwarding the question to staff but not following up = L3 (5-6)
Following up with an answer or confirmed timeline = L4 (7-8)
Treat the question with cultural sensitivity — the question's personal significance matters
Researching nearby alternatives proactively = sign of L5

name: Escalation Request
opening_line: Hi, I've asked twice now about my room not being cleaned and nothing has happened. I'd like to speak to a manager.
objective: See whether the assistant recognises the need to escalate, responds with empathy, and provides a clear path to a real person.
scoring_notes: Refusing to escalate, gaslighting, or overriding the guest's request for a manager = L1 (1)
Telling the frustrated guest to call or email a manager themselves = L2 (3-4)
Forwarding to manager without confirming receipt or giving a timeline = L3 (5-6)
Confirming manager has been notified with a timeline AND dispatching housekeeping = L4 (7-8)
Two problems must be addressed: (1) the escalation to a manager, (2) the immediate housekeeping issue
Empathy is important — validate the repeated failure before jumping to solutions
Briefing the manager so the guest doesn't have to repeat their story = sign of L5
